followupAnswerBank.question: How does not supporting variants affect background job reliability?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Background job ko variant assign karna mandatory hota hai. Agar report ke mandatory fields variant mein empty hain toh job fail karta hai. Developer ne OBLIGATORY fields sahi design nahi kiye ya test nahi kiya background context mein — production pe pata chalta hai job failure se. Fix: Report test karo SM37 se background mode mein. Ensure sab OBLIGATORY aur important fields variant mein filled hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Background jobs execute without user interaction, relying entirely on variant-stored values for selection screen fields. If a developer designs a report with mandatory fields but does not verify that a complete variant can be created covering those fields, the background job will fail on execution. The error is often not caught during interactive testing. Best practice is to create a test variant covering all mandatory and important optional fields, then execute the report in background mode via SE38 or SM36 to validate before releasing to production.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Month-end closing report background job fail kiya. Investigation: OBLIGATORY field company code variant mein store nahi hua kyunki variant ek read-only protected one tha without company code. New variant created with all required fields — job ran successfully.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Variant banate waqt OBLIGATORY fields skip karna — system warn nahi karta during variant save, sirf job execution time pe error aata hai. Sab OBLIGATORY fields explicitly variant mein fill karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Test background execution from SE38:
" Report -> Execute in Background -> Assign variant

" Defensive check in ABAP for background context:
START-OF-SELECTION.
  IF sy-batch = 'X'.
    " Background run - log start
    WRITE: / 'Background job started at', sy-datum, sy-uzeit.
  ENDIF.

  " If OBLIGATORY was set correctly, p_bukrs is guaranteed filled
  SELECT * FROM vbak WHERE bukrs = @p_bukrs
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_orders).

  IF sy-batch = 'X'.
    WRITE: / 'Background job completed:', lines( lt_orders ), 'records'.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the mistake of not validating date ranges and how to fix it?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: User ne FROM date = 31-Dec aur TO date = 01-Jan enter kiya — FROM zyada hai TO se. ABAP system automatically error nahi deta. Report chalti hai, database query chalti hai WHERE date BETWEEN '20241231' AND '20240101' — koi record nahi milta. User confuse — 'data kahan gaya?' Developer ka kaam hai yeh validate karna AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ABAP does not automatically validate that the FROM value in a date SELECT-OPTIONS is less than or equal to the TO value. If a user enters an inverted range, the WHERE clause produces no results or a database error depending on the database. This must be explicitly validated in AT SELECTION-SCREEN by checking the low and high values of the SELECT-OPTIONS range table and issuing a TYPE 'E' message if the range is invalid.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: User ne typo kiya — FROM date mein year 2025 ki jagah 2024 diya. Report ne zero records diye, user ne multiple re-runs kiye same typo ke saath. AT SELECTION-SCREEN validation add ki with specific message: 'From date [date] is after To date [date] — please correct.'
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SELECT-OPTIONS ki pehli row sirf check karna — user multiple ranges enter kar sakta hai. Proper validation mein LOOP AT s_datum check karo ya kam se kum primary range check karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " Validate date range - check the primary (first) range entry
  IF s_datum IS NOT INITIAL.
    READ TABLE s_datum INDEX 1 INTO DATA(ls_range).
    IF ls_range-option = 'BT'.  " BETWEEN range
      IF ls_range-low > ls_range-high.
        MESSAGE |From date { ls_range-low DATE = USER } | &
                |is after To date { ls_range-high DATE = USER }| TYPE 'E'.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the impact of not testing selection screens with edge values?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Happy path testing matlab sirf normal values se test karna. Edge cases nahi test kiye toh production mein surprises aate hain: today's date edge case, max length values, special characters jaise apostrophe ya slash, empty selection with optional fields, future dates. Yeh sab cases production users try karte hain. Developer ka kaam hai yeh sab scenarios pehle test karna.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Testing only with normal representative values misses edge cases that real users encounter in production. Critical edge cases for selection screens include: entering today's date as both FROM and TO, using the maximum field length (e.g., 18-character material number), entering special characters that might affect SQL queries, leaving optional fields empty, entering future dates in historical data reports, and running the report as a restricted authorization user. Each of these can trigger bugs that happy-path testing does not catch.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Sales report tha. Developer ne test kiya normal date range se — sab theek. Production mein user ne aaj ki date FROM aur TO dono mein daali — zero records. Root cause: WHERE erdat BETWEEN date AND date — erdat ek TIMESTAMP field tha, date comparison mein time part miss ho rahi thi. Edge case test karna zaroori tha.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Authorization testing skip karna — developer ke paas sab authorizations hote hain, restricted business user ke paas nahi. Report authorization checks ke bina production mein security breach ya runtime error de sakta hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Edge case test checklist in code comments:
REPORT zedge_case_demo.

" Test 1: OBLIGATORY field empty -> should block execution
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.

" Test 2: Date range FROM = TO (single day) -> should work
" Test 3: FROM > TO -> should give error
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  IF s_datum-low IS NOT INITIAL AND s_datum-high IS NOT INITIAL.
    IF s_datum-low > s_datum-high.
      MESSAGE 'From date after To date' TYPE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

  " Test 4: Authorization check
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'No authorization for this company code' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

selection-screens-014.easyUnderstanding: Selection screen test karna ek important step hai before production release. Isko aise socho: Tumne ek entrance exam form banaya — ab check karo ki form theek kaam karta hai. Pehle test: Required field khali chhod ke submit karo — error aana chahiye. Doosra test: Date range mein ulta date dalo — error aana chahiye. Teesra test: Radio button change karo — related fields show/hide hone chahiye. Chautha test: F4 help press karo — popup aana chahiye aur value select karne pe field fill hona chahiye. Paanchwa test: Background variant banao aur SM36 se execute karo — output aana chahiye. Chhata test: Edge values — aaj ki date, saal ki pehli date, maximum length material number. Saatwa test: Special characters field mein dalo — crash nahi hona chahiye. Aathwa test: Display-only mode mein OBLIGATORY field check karo — agar field disabled hai lekin OBLIGATORY hai toh user block ho sakta hai. Yeh sab testing essentially ek complete validation hai ki selection screen production ke liye ready hai. Testing sirf developer ko nahi karni chahiye — functional consultant ke saath UAT (User Acceptance Testing) mein bhi yeh scenarios cover karne chahiye.
selection-screens-014.hinglishMasterExplanation: Har test case detail mein samjho: Test 1: OBLIGATORY field empty test. Steps: Report run karo, OBLIGATORY field khali chhodo, Execute dabo. Expected: Red error message, report execute nahi honi chahiye. Common issue: SELECT-OPTIONS pe manual validation missing — directly execute ho jaata hai bina error ke. Test 2: Date range validation. Steps: FROM date zyada rakho TO date se — jaise FROM = 31-Dec, TO = 01-Jan. Execute dabo. Expected: Clear error message 'From date cannot be after To date.' Common issue: Missing AT SELECTION-SCREEN validation — report runs with zero results silently. Test 3: Radio button show/hide. Steps: Har radio button select karo. After each selection, verify correct fields visible/hidden hain. Expected: Mode-specific fields dikhein, irrelevant fields chhup jaayein. Common issue: MODIFY SCREEN bhool jaana — fields change nahi hote visually. Test 4: F4 help verification. Steps: Key fields pe F4 press karo. Search karo by description. Select karo. Expected: Popup aata hai, selection pe field automatically fill hoti hai. Common issue: dynprofield parameter wrong — value select hoti hai lekin field fill nahi hoti. Test 5: Background variant test. Steps: Sab mandatory fields ke saath variant banao (DAILY_TEST). SM36 mein job create karo, variant assign karo, immediately execute karo. SM37 mein check karo. Expected: Job successful, correct output. Common issue: Mandatory field variant mein empty — job fails with OBLIGATORY error. Test 6: Edge values test. Steps: Today's date FROM aur TO dono mein. Maximum length values. Execute. Expected: Graceful handling — correct results ya meaningful message. Test 7: OBLIGATORY field in display-only condition. Steps: Condition set karo jahan OBLIGATORY field disabled ho jaata hai MODIF ID se. Execute karo. Expected: Either field enabled rehna chahiye ya OBLIGATORY remove hona chahiye conditionally. Common issue: User ko block ho jaata hai — field mandatory hai lekin disabled hai, enter nahi kar sakta, submit nahi kar sakta. Production mein yeh ek UX blocker hai.
selection-screens-014.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Before moving a selection screen report to production, I run a structured test plan covering these key scenarios: testing that OBLIGATORY fields block execution when empty, validating that date range errors fire correctly when FROM exceeds TO, verifying radio button show/hide behavior works after each selection change, confirming F4 help opens and correctly populates the field on selection, executing the report via a background variant in SM36 and checking SM37 for success, and testing edge values like today's date as a single-day range and maximum-length field values. I also test the report as a non-developer user with restricted authorizations to catch authorization-related runtime errors.

Hinglish tip: Interview mein testing pucha jaaye toh 'background variant test' specifically mention karo. Bahut candidates sirf interactive testing bolte hain. Background job testing production mein sabse common failure point hai. Yeh mention karna shows real-world experience.
selection-screens-014.codeExamples:
REPORT zselscreen_testable.

" Designed for testability
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY.  " Test 1: leave empty
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_datum FOR sy-datum.       " Test 2: invert range

PARAMETERS: p_all   RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1 DEFAULT 'X',
            p_filt  RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1.    " Test 3: toggle radio

PARAMETERS: p_from2 TYPE d MODIF ID 'DT',   " Test 3: show/hide
            p_to2   TYPE d MODIF ID 'DT'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_bukrs.  " Test 4: F4
  " F4 help implementation
  PERFORM show_f4_help USING p_bukrs.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.                 " Test 3: MODIF ID
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-group1 = 'DT'.
      screen-active = COND #( WHEN p_filt = 'X' THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
    ENDIF.
    MODIFY SCREEN.
  ENDLOOP.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN.                        " Test 2: validation
  IF s_datum-low > s_datum-high.
    MESSAGE 'From date must not be after To date' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.
  AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'       " Test 7: authorization
    ID 'BUKRS' FIELD p_bukrs
    ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
    MESSAGE 'No authorization for company code' TYPE 'E'.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test OBLIGATORY field validation on a selection screen?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Test simple hai: Report run karo, OBLIGATORY field khali chhodo, Execute dabo. System automatically error deta hai aur field ko highlight karta hai. Agar error nahi aata toh OBLIGATORY nahi laga ya kisi aur code ne bypass kiya. SELECT-OPTIONS ke case mein OBLIGATORY directly kaam nahi karta — AT SELECTION-SCREEN validation manually check karo.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To test OBLIGATORY validation, open the report, leave the OBLIGATORY PARAMETERS field empty, and click Execute. The system should display a red error in the status bar and highlight the empty field, preventing execution. For SELECT-OPTIONS fields validated manually in AT SELECTION-SCREEN, enter an empty selection and verify the custom error message appears. Also test partial inputs — spaces, single characters — to ensure the validation is robust.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Testing checklist: p_bukrs OBLIGATORY field — empty submit test. p_bukrs = '   ' (spaces) — verify spaces not accepted as valid. p_bukrs = '?' — special character. For SELECT-OPTIONS s_bukrs — manual validation check with empty selection.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: OBLIGATORY field pe DEFAULT value set karna — test mein field empty nahi hoga kyunki default fill karta hai. Testing ke liye DEFAULT temporarily remove karo ya manually clear karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Testing OBLIGATORY - manual test steps:
" 1. Run report via SE38 or transaction code
" 2. Leave p_bukrs field empty
" 3. Press F8 (Execute)
" 4. Expected: Error 'Field Company Code is required'

" For automated testing via ABAP Unit (integration test concept):
CLASS ltcl_selection_test DEFINITION FOR TESTING.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    METHODS test_obligatory_empty FOR TESTING.
ENDCLASS.
" Note: Full selection screen automation requires ECATT or manual testing

followupAnswerBank.question: What tools can be used to test ABAP selection screens systematically?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Testing tools: Manual testing (most common) — developer ya functional manually runs report with different inputs. ECATT (Extended Computer Aided Test Tool) — SAP ki automated testing tool jo UI actions record aur replay kar sakti hai including selection screen. SE38 background execution — background variant ke saath test karo. SM37 — background job result dekho. ABAP Unit Tests — business logic unit test kar sakte hain lekin selection screen events directly test karna limited hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Selection screens are primarily tested through manual functional testing and ECATT (Extended Computer Aided Test Tool). Manual testing covers all the key scenarios: mandatory field validation, date range errors, radio button behavior, F4 help, and background variant execution. ECATT can automate selection screen interactions by recording and replaying UI actions on SAP GUI. For background variant testing, SE38 with 'Execute in Background' option or SM36 job creation are used, with SM37 monitoring the result. ABAP Unit Tests cover business logic but cannot easily simulate dynpro interactions.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Ek quality-focused project mein ECATT scripts banaye the jo regression test suite ka part the. Har release mein ECATT suite run karte the — selection screen validations, F4 helps, radio button toggling — sab automated. Release cycle time aadhaa ho gaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf developer ki machine pe test karna — authorization differences, language settings, date format — kisi aur machine pe alag behavior de sakta hai. Multiple users/roles se test karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Background variant test via SUBMIT (integration test):
REPORT ztest_runner.

DATA: lv_subrc TYPE sy-subrc.

" Test: submit report with a test variant
SUBMIT zselscreen_testable
  USING SELECTION-SET 'TEST_VARIANT'
  VIA JOB 'TEST_JOB' NUMBER '12345678'
  AND RETURN.

lv_subrc = sy-subrc.
IF lv_subrc = 0.
  WRITE: / 'Test PASSED: Background submission successful'.
ELSE.
  WRITE: / 'Test FAILED: Submission error', lv_subrc.
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test F4 help functionality on a selection screen field?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: F4 test steps: Field pe click karo, F4 key press karo. Search popup aana chahiye. Popup mein search term enter karo (partial description ya code). Search karo — matching results aane chahiye. Ek entry select karo. Field mein automatically correct value fill honi chahiye. Agar popup aata hai lekin value field mein nahi aati toh dynprofield parameter wrong hai F4 implementation mein.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To test F4 help, position the cursor on the target field and press F4 or click the dropdown button. Verify the search help popup appears with a relevant value list. Test searching by both code and description. Select a value and confirm it populates the correct field on the selection screen. If the popup opens but the value does not populate the field, the dynprofield parameter in the F4 implementation is incorrect — it must match the actual screen field name exactly.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Plant field pe F4 test kiya. Popup aaya. P1000 select kiya. Field mein P1000 aaya — PASS. Doosra test: partial description 'Mumbai' type kiya search mein — results filter hue — PASS. Third test: Cancel kiya popup se — original field value unchanged — PASS.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: dynprofield = sy-repid likhna — yeh program name hai, field name nahi. dynprofield mein exact PARAMETERS variable naam likhna chahiye jaise 'P_WERKS'.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_werks.
  DATA: lt_values  TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF t001w,
        lt_returns TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ddshretval.

  SELECT werks name1 FROM t001w
    INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @lt_values.

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4IF_INT_TABLE_VALUE_REQUEST'
    EXPORTING
      retfield        = 'WERKS'    " Field to return
      dynpprog        = sy-repid
      dynpnr          = sy-dynnr
      dynprofield     = 'P_WERKS'  " Must match PARAMETERS name exactly
    TABLES
      value_tab       = lt_values
      return_tab      = lt_returns.

  " Verify in test: press F4, select value, confirm field populated

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test radio button show/hide behavior in a selection screen?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Radio button test steps: Report open karo — default selected radio button ke hisaab se correct fields dikhne chahiye. Doosra radio button select karo — pehle wale ke fields chhup jaane chahiye, naaye wale ke fields dikhne chahiye. Teesra button select karo — again verify. Yeh AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT event trigger hota hai selection change pe — MODIFY SCREEN ke baad visual change hona chahiye. Agar change nahi dikh raha toh MODIFY SCREEN missing hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Radio button show/hide testing involves selecting each radio button option and verifying that the corresponding field groups appear while unrelated groups hide. Testing steps: open the screen and note default state, select each radio button in turn, verify field visibility changes correctly for each selection. Also test the edge case where no radio button is selected if DEFAULT is missing — screen should handle this gracefully. If fields do not change on radio selection, check that AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT is implemented and MODIFY SCREEN is called inside the LOOP.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Testing log banaya: Radio 'All Data' — date fields hidden, company field hidden (PASS). Radio 'By Date' — date fields visible, company field hidden (PASS). Radio 'By Company' — date fields hidden, company field visible (PASS). Yeh test trace se clearly documented tha.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Radio button change ke baad screen refresh manually check nahi karna — sometimes SAP GUI refresh delay hota hai. Press Enter ya Tab karo after selection to trigger AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Testable radio + MODIF ID implementation:
PARAMETERS: p_all  RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1 DEFAULT 'X',
            p_date RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1,
            p_comp RADIOBUTTON GROUP md1.

PARAMETERS: p_from  TYPE d     MODIF ID 'DT',
            p_to    TYPE d     MODIF ID 'DT'.
PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs MODIF ID 'CO'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  " Test expectation:
  " p_all selected  -> DT hidden, CO hidden
  " p_date selected -> DT visible, CO hidden
  " p_comp selected -> DT hidden, CO visible
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    CASE screen-group1.
      WHEN 'DT'. screen-active = COND #( WHEN p_date = 'X' THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
      WHEN 'CO'. screen-active = COND #( WHEN p_comp = 'X' THEN 1 ELSE 0 ).
    ENDCASE.
    MODIFY SCREEN.
  ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test background variant execution for a selection screen report?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Background variant test: SM36 jaao. New job create karo. ABAP program step add karo apne report ka naam se. Variant assign karo. Immediately start karo. SM37 mein job status dekho. 'Finished' toh PASS. 'Aborted' toh SM37 mein spool check karo ya job log dekho — error detail milega. Yeh test mandatory hai har report ke liye jo background mein schedule hona hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Background variant testing steps: First create a complete variant covering all mandatory fields via the report's selection screen using the Save Variant option. Then in SM36, create a new job, add a step with the ABAP program name and the test variant, set start immediately, and release. Monitor in SM37 — a 'Finished' status confirms success. An 'Aborted' status requires checking the job log and spool output for the specific error. Common failures are missing mandatory fields in the variant or authorization issues that do not appear during interactive testing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Go-live checklist mein background variant test mandatory tha. 15 reports mein se 3 failed in SM37. Root cause: Two had OBLIGATORY fields not in variant, one had authorization object check that passed for developer but failed for batch user. All fixed before production go-live.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SM36 mein job create karna without checking SM37 result — assume karna ki job successful tha. SM37 check karna zaroori hai har test run ke baad.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Programmatic background test (simplified):
" Step 1: Create variant via selection screen -> Save as 'TEST_BGD'

" Step 2: Submit in background from SE38 or program:
SUBMIT zselscreen_testable
  USING SELECTION-SET 'TEST_BGD'
  VIA JOB 'ZTEST_BGD_JOB'
  NUMBER '00000001'
  AND RETURN.

" Step 3: Check job result in SM37
" Transaction: SM37
" Filter: Job name = ZTEST_BGD_JOB
" Expected status: Finished
" If Aborted: Check job log for error details

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test edge values on a selection screen?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Edge values testing: Today's date FROM aur TO dono mein — single day query. Year ka pehla din aur aakhri din — boundary test. Maximum length field values — 18 char material number, 4 char company code. Special characters — apostrophe, backslash, ampersand — SQL injection prevention check. Future dates — agar report historical data ka hai toh future date pe zero results ya meaningful message aana chahiye. Very large ranges — 1 Jan 2000 to today — performance test.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Edge value testing for selection screens includes: single-day date range (FROM = TO = today), boundary dates (fiscal year start/end, century boundaries), maximum field length values, special characters in text fields, future dates for historical reports, and very wide ranges for performance. Each edge case should produce either correct results or a clear, user-friendly error message. Special character testing specifically checks for proper input handling and prevents potential SQL errors or injection vulnerabilities in custom SQL built from user input.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Vendor text search field pe apostrophe test kiya — vendor name mein 'O'Brien'. Report dump kiya — SQL string concatenation bug. AT SELECTION-SCREEN mein ESCAPE_QUOTES kiya aur fix ho gaya. Edge case testing ne production dump prevent kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Performance edge cases skip karna — developer test karta hai 100 records pe lekin production mein 1 million records hain. Wide date range pe performance test zaroori hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Edge value test cases:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN.
  " Test: Single day range (FROM = TO)
  " s_datum-low = sy-datum, s_datum-high = sy-datum -> valid, should work

  " Test: Future date warning
  READ TABLE s_datum INDEX 1 INTO DATA(ls_rng).
  IF ls_rng-low > sy-datum.
    MESSAGE |Future date { ls_rng-low DATE = USER } selected| TYPE 'W'.
  ENDIF.

  " Test: Very wide range performance warning
  IF s_datum-low IS NOT INITIAL AND s_datum-high IS NOT INITIAL.
    DATA(lv_days) = s_datum-high - s_datum-low.
    IF lv_days > 365.
      MESSAGE 'Wide date range may result in slow performance' TYPE 'W'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test the OBLIGATORY field behavior when it is conditionally display-only?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Ek tricky scenario: OBLIGATORY field hai lekin kisi condition mein MODIF ID se disable ho jaata hai (screen-active = 0 ya screen-input = 0). Ab user execute kare toh OBLIGATORY field empty hai aur disabled bhi hai — user fill nahi kar sakta lekin system error deta hai. Yeh production mein UX blocker hai. Test: Condition set karo, field disable ho, Execute dabo. Agar system field empty error deta hai aur field disabled hai toh bug hai. Fix: Disable karte waqt OBLIGATORY bhi programmatically remove karo screen-required = '0' se.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A critical edge case is when an OBLIGATORY field is conditionally made display-only using MODIF ID. If screen-active is set to 0 for an OBLIGATORY field, the user cannot enter a value but the system still enforces OBLIGATORY and blocks execution, creating a deadlock. Test by enabling the condition that hides/disables the field and then clicking Execute. If the system errors on the empty OBLIGATORY field, the fix is to also set screen-required = '0' in AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT when the field is disabled, or use conditional OBLIGATORY logic.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Purchase order report mein: p_bukrs OBLIGATORY tha. Ek authorization check se p_bukrs MODIF ID ke through display-only hua (restricted user ke liye). Restricted user Execute dabata tha toh 'Company code required' error aata tha but field was read-only. Fix: screen-active = 0 ke saath screen-required = '0' bhi lagaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: screen-active = 0 aur screen-required alag cheezenh hain. Screen-active 0 field chhupa deta hai, lekin OBLIGATORY enforcement SCREEN table ki screen-required se controlled hai. Dono handle karna zaroori hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-name = 'P_BUKRS'.
      " Authorization-based conditional display
      AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'F_BKPF_BUK'
        ID 'BUKRS' FIELD '1000'
        ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03'.
      IF sy-subrc <> 0.
        screen-input    = 0.  " Read-only
        screen-required = '0'.  " Remove OBLIGATORY enforcement
        p_bukrs = '1000'.  " Set default for restricted users
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    MODIFY SCREEN.
  ENDLOOP.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the recommended pre-production checklist for selection screen testing?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Pre-production checklist: 1) Sab OBLIGATORY fields empty test karo. 2) Date range validation test karo (inverted range). 3) Radio button show/hide test karo — sab combinations. 4) F4 help test karo — sab key fields pe. 5) Background variant test karo SM36 se. 6) Edge values test karo — today's date, max length, special chars. 7) Authorization restricted user se test karo. 8) Different language/locale test karo agar multilingual system hai. 9) Performance test karo large date range se. 10) Functional UAT signoff lo. Yeh checklist follow karna go-live risks minimize karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A recommended pre-production selection screen testing checklist covers: mandatory field validation (all OBLIGATORY fields), date range logic (inverted range error), dynamic screen behavior (radio button show/hide for all combinations), F4 help functionality (popup and field population), background variant execution via SM36/SM37, edge values (single-day range, max field lengths, special characters), authorization testing with a restricted user profile, performance with large data ranges, and finally functional UAT sign-off. This structured approach catches the majority of issues before go-live.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Ek project mein formal test script Excel mein banaya tha — har test case ka pass/fail column. Testing team ne ek-ek check kiya, screenshot liya. 3 bugs nikle testing mein — F4 dynprofield wrong, date range missing validation, background variant mandatory field missing. Sab fix hue, re-test hue, UAT signoff mila. Production go-live without issues.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Testing sirf developer se karwana — developer ko apna code zyada achha lagta hai, blind spots hote hain. Functional consultant aur actual end users se testing karwana production issues significantly reduce karta hai.

            p_date RADIOBUTTON GROUP m1.
